trivia_bullets.text: Part of the Blighted cycle — five colorless utility lands in Battle for Zendikar (BFZ, released 2015-10-02), one per color, each producing {C} plus a sacrificial colored-ability. Blighted Steppe is the white member: sacrifice plus {3}{W} to gain 2 life per creature you control. The cycle was designed to give each color a land-based version of that color's characteristic effect without needing the land to produce colored mana (adding colored mana would have required an enters-tapped clause and made the cards too wordy). [Scryfall; WebSearch: MythicSpoiler Blighted cycle design note]
sources: WebSearch: MythicSpoiler BFZ land cycle design

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "When a limb is gangrenous, you cut it off. This is no different." — Greenweaver Mina. Greenweaver Mina is an unnamed Zendikari character; 'greenweaver' is likely a title indicating a druid or nature-magic practitioner. The quote frames the blighting of Zendikar's steppes as triage logic — sacrificing corrupted land to preserve the whole — mirroring the card's sacrifice mechanic. [Scryfall: BFZ-232 flavor_text]

EDHREC rank 7,865 as of 2026-05-19 — the most Commander-played card in this six-card BFZ batch. Sees use in white token and lifegain Commander decks that can scale the life-gain effect with creature count, but considered a fringe inclusion given the colorless mana production and four-mana activation cost. Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall: legalities; EDHREC rank from Scryfall edhrec_rank field]
